Title: How to Add a PS4 Player as a Friend in Fortnite on PC Introduction: In this comprehensive guide, you will learn how to add a PlayStation 4 (PS4) player as a friend in Fortnite while playing on your personal computer (PC). Cross-platform compatibility allows players from different systems to connect and play together. This tutorial covers all necessary steps with clear explanations and helpful tips. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Make sure both accounts have Epic Games account linking enabled for crossplay functionality. * Be aware that communication between platforms is limited; voice chat requires separate setup or third-party software. Step 1: Create an Epic Games Account If you don't already have one, create an Epic Games account at <https://www.epicgames.com/> by clicking 'Sign Up.' An Epic Games account acts as the unified identity across all devices including PS4, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch, mobile, and PC. Step 2: Link Your Console Account Link your console account (in this case, PSN) to your Epic Games account following these sub-steps: a. Log into your Epic Games account on their website. b. Navigate to 'Account Settings,' then select 'Connections.' c. Locate the PlayStation Network logo and click 'Connect.' You may be prompted to log into your PSN account if not logged in already. Follow on-screen prompts to complete the process. Step 3: Launch Fortnite on Your PC Start Fortnite using either the Epic Games launcher or any supported third-party platform like Steam. Step 4: Access Social Menu Once in the game lobby, locate and open the social menu. The icon typically resembles three people or heads. On keyboard input, this can often be accessed through the default shortcut 'O'. Step 5: Search for Friends Within the social menu, find and click the option labeled 'Search.' Enter the exact display name of the PS4 player you wish to add. Remember that usernames are case sensitive. Step 6: Send Friend Request After finding the correct username, send them a friend request via the appropriate button ('ADD FRIEND', etc.). A notification should appear confirming the successful sending of the friend request. Step 7: Wait for Confirmation The final step involves waiting for the other party to accept your friend request. Once they do so, you will see them listed under your friends list within the social menu. Troubleshooting Common Issues: Issue: Cannot find the person despite entering the right username. Solution: Double check spelling, capitalization, and spaces in the entered username. If still unable to locate them, ensure they have properly linked their PSN account to their Epic Games profile. Issue: Received error stating crossplay must be enabled. Solution: Check both your own settings and those of the intended recipient. Both parties need to enable crossplay functionality in order to interact. Conclusion: By diligently following these steps, adding a PS4 player as a friend in Fortnite on your PC becomes straightforward. Enjoy seamless gaming experiences alongside friends regardless of what system they choose! yeah, he is so obsessed with those things. Title: Understanding How to Optimize Solutions with Helpful Tools Have you ever played a game where you had to find the best way to move objects around to achieve the highest score or the fastest time? Well, just like how we use strategies to win games, mathematicians use tools to find the best solutions to problems! One of these tools is called "Lagrange Multipliers." Let's imagine you are trying to fit two pieces of string, one red and one blue, into a rectangular box without any overlap. The red string must touch both longer sides of the box, while the blue string must touch both shorter sides (see the diagram). Your goal is to make the strings as long as possible while still fitting inside the box. [Insert Diagram] To solve this problem, you could think of it as optimizing the lengths of the strings ($x$ and $y$) subject to certain conditions ($g_1$, $g_2$, and $g_3$). These conditions ensure that the strings stay within the boundaries of the box and meet the requirements mentioned earlier. Now, here comes the cool part! You can use something called "Lagrange Multipliers" to simultaneously satisfy all these constraints and find the optimal lengths for your strings. Essentially, you create a new function, which combines your original objective function and constraint functions, and then find the points where their gradients (rate of change) are equal to zero. This helps identify potential maximum or minimum points. For our string example, you would set up equations similar to those described in the webpage snippet provided but avoid using complex math symbols. By solving them together, you will discover the ideal lengths for the red and blue strings, making sure they perfectly fill the box without overlapping. However, there's more to this technique than simply plugging numbers into equations. Once you have identified possible optimum points, you should always double-check whether they indeed represent maxima or minima. To do so, examine the behavior of the original objective function near each point - its curvature should confirm whether it's a maximum, minimum, or saddle point. In summary, understanding and utilizing techniques like Lagrange Multipliers allows us to tackle complex problems involving multiple constraints efficiently. Just like winning a game requires smart strategy, mastering advanced mathematical methods opens doors to solving challenging real-world issues! I know you said the spaghetti would probably... [wipes Chi's base on her pants leg to clean it] Title: How to Clean the Velcro Strips on a Swiffer Floor Mop The Swiffer floor mop is a popular tool for quick and easy cleaning around the house. However, over time, the Velcro strips that attach the reusable cloths can become dirty or clogged with debris, reducing their effectiveness. This guide will provide you with a detailed, step-by-step process on how to clean the Velcro strips on your Swiffer floor mop, ensuring optimal performance. **Things You'll Need:** 1. A used toothbrush or small brush 2. Dish soap 3. Warm water 4. Dry towel 5. Scissors (optional) ### **Step 1: Gather Your Materials** Before starting the cleaning process, ensure you have all necessary materials at hand. Having everything ready will make the task more efficient and less frustrating. ### **Step 2: Remove Clinging Debris** Start by removing any large pieces of dirt, hair, or other debris stuck to the Velcro strips using your fingers or tweezers. Be gentle so as not to damage the strip. Removing these visible particles first makes subsequent steps easier. ### **Step 3: Brush the Velcro Strips** Use a soft-bristled toothbrush or small brush to gently scrub along the length of the Velcro strip. The brushing motion helps dislodge smaller particles embedded within the hooks of the Velcro. For best results, hold the Swiffer mop upside down so gravity assists in freeing trapped debris. ### **Step 4: Soak in Soapy Water** Fill a container with warm water and add a few drops of dish soap. Submerge the Velcro strips in the solution for about five minutes. Soaking loosens stubborn grime and prepares the strips for thorough cleaning. ### **Step 5: Agitate the Velcro Strands** After soaking, agitate the Velcro strands under running water. Use your finger or the brush to rub against the grain of the Velcro, further dislodging accumulated dust and dirt. Rinse thoroughly until the water runs clear. ### **Step 6: Dry Completely** Pat the Velcro strips dry with a towel before letting them air dry completely. It's crucial that they are entirely dried out before reattaching them to the mop head; otherwise, mold could grow. Avoid using heat sources like hairdryers, which might warp the plastic components. ### **Step 7: Reattach the Velcro Strips** Once fully dried, reattach the Velcro strips to the mop head. If some parts seem stretched out or misshapen after washing, trim them carefully with scissors. Misaligned sections may affect adhesion quality. ### **Key Tips and Guidelines:** - Regularly maintain your Swiffer mop according to this guide to prolong its lifespan and efficiency. - Always check the manufacturer's recommendations regarding washability and care procedures. - Never forcefully pull off stuck items from the Velcro strips, as doing so could cause irreversible damage. - Don't immerse electrical parts of the Swiffer mop in water or any liquid. - Consider replacing worn-out or heavily damaged Velcro strips if cleaning doesn't improve their functionality. Title: How to Deal with Mass Quantities of Paperwork Introduction: Paperwork is an inevitable part of many professions and personal tasks. When dealing with large volumes of paper documents, managing and organizing them efficiently can become challenging. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on handling mass quantities of paperwork while maintaining accuracy, efficiency, and organization. Step 1: Setting Up Your Workspace Create a dedicated workspace that allows sufficient room for sorting, filing, and storing your paperwork. Keep this area clutter-free, well lit, and equipped with necessary supplies like file folders, labels, pens, sticky notes, and a shredder or recycling bin. A clean workspace increases productivity and reduces errors by minimizing distractions. Key Tip: Establishing a proper posture when working prevents fatigue and strain injuries. Use an adjustable chair and ensure your computer monitor is at eye level. Step 2: Prioritization Sort through the pile of papers and categorize them based on their urgency, importance, and deadlines. You may create categories such as 'Immediate Action', 'Follow up Later', 'To File', 'To Read' etc. Prioritizing ensures critical documents receive prompt attention, reducing potential risks associated with delayed processing. Guideline: Consider using color-coded files or sticky tabs for easy visual identification of priority levels. Step 3: Initial Sorting Divide the entire batch into smaller manageable sections (e.g., daily portions). Breaking down the volume makes the task less overwhelming and enables better focus. Start with one section, going page by page, taking action according to predefined categories from Step 2. Key Tip: Avoid multitasking during this process; focusing solely on the current document improves concentration and speeds up completion time. Step 4: Document Processing Process each document systematically following its category. For instance, if it falls under 'Immediate Action', take care of any required actions immediately before moving onto the next document. If unsure about what needs doing, jot down questions or next steps on a separate notepad. Guideline: Implement a standardized approach for recording decisions made on each document, whether via digital tools or handwritten logs. Step 5: Filing System Implement a logical and consistent filing system. Decide on broad categories first (like clients, projects, departments), then break these down further into subcategories. Label all files clearly and alphabetically or numerically where possible. Digital scanning and storage solutions are also worth considering here. Key Tip: Regularly update your filing system to reflect changes in your workload or responsibilities. Outdated systems lead to misplaced documents and wasted time searching. Step 6: Routine Maintenance Set aside regular time slots weekly or monthly to review and maintain your paperwork management routine. Clear out completed tasks, update records, purge unnecessary documents, and reorganize as needed. Consistent maintenance saves future headaches caused by disarrayed records. Guideline: Develop a habit of date tracking - either manually added to physical files or automatically recorded within digital platforms. It simplifies retrieval and archiving processes significantly. Conclusion: Managing mass quantities of paperwork effectively requires discipline, organization, and proactive measures. By implementing the strategies discussed above, you should be able to handle vast amounts of documentation proficiently, thereby improving overall professional performance and personal satisfaction. Title: How to Get a Guy Who is Experiencing Strong Breakup Trauma Introduction: Breakups can be incredibly difficult and traumatic experiences for anyone involved. If you have feelings for someone going through this kind of pain, reaching out and supporting them could be beneficial for both parties. However, it's essential to approach such situations with sensitivity, patience, and understanding. This guide will provide an in-depth explanation of steps and strategies that can help you get close to a guy who has experienced strong breakup trauma while ensuring his emotional wellbeing remains paramount throughout the process. Step 1: Educate Yourself About Breakup Trauma Before taking any action, educate yourself about what breakup trauma entails. It includes symptoms like depression, anxiety, mood swings, difficulty sleeping or eating, lack of motivation, and social withdrawal. Understanding these signs will allow you to empathize better and respond appropriately when interacting with him. Key Tip: Be aware that everyone heals differently; therefore, respect his pace during recovery. Step 2: Establish Contact Reach out to him via text message, call, email, or even face-to-face conversation if possible. Let him know you care and are there for him without expecting anything in return. A simple 'Hello, I hope you're doing okay,' can open up communication channels. Guideline: Avoid discussing his ex or their relationship at first. Focus on making him feel comfortable opening up to you. Step 3: Listen Actively When he starts talking about his feelings, listen attentively. Show genuine interest by asking relevant questions but refrain from offering unsolicited advice. Just being present and listening may provide significant comfort. Key Point: Active listening demonstrates your support and builds trust between you two. Step 4: Offer Help Subtly If he seems receptive to your company, suggest activities together that might distract him from negative thoughts. These could include watching movies, playing sports, cooking, hiking, etc., depending on his interests. Remember, no pressure! Step 5: Encourage Professional Help If his condition worsens over time (e.g., prolonged sadness, suicidal ideation), encourage professional assistance. Therapists specializing in relationships and grief counseling can offer specialized guidance tailored to his needs. Step 6: Give Him Space While maintaining contact is important, also give him space to heal privately. Overwhelming him with constant attention could lead to unnecessary stress. Balance is crucial here. Step 7: Gradually Express Your Feelings Once you notice improvements in his mental state, slowly express your romantic feelings towards him. Use indirect methods initially, such as mentioning shared memories or complimenting aspects unrelated to appearance. Key Guideline: Timing is everything. Make sure he feels ready before sharing your emotions. Step 8: Handle Rejection Gracefully In case he doesn't reciprocate your feelings due to personal reasons or ongoing healing processes, handle it gracefully. Respect his decision and maintain the friendship, unless it becomes too painful for either party. Conclusion: Helping a guy dealing with strong breakup trauma requires compassion, patience, and tactfulness. By following these steps, you create opportunities to develop a deep bond built upon mutual respect, understanding, and affection. Always remember though, every person's journey through heartache is unique, so adapt these suggestions accordingly. She won't be able to drive it no not 'til Papa fixes it. [Home Visit 1; takes place on living room carpet surrounded by toys from preceding toy play; Bro and father and two neighborhood children are also in room] Title: How to Change Your Trading Name as a Sole Trader Introduction: Changing your trading name as a sole trader can be an exciting endeavor, especially when you're revamping your brand or expanding into new markets. This guide will walk you through the process, providing clear steps and useful insights along the way. By following these instructions, you'll ensure that your transition is smooth and compliant with legal requirements. Step 1: Choose a New Trading Name Before changing your trading name, take time to brainstorm ideas that align with your business values, target audience, and growth plans. Consider consulting with professionals such as marketing experts or graphic designers to create a strong brand identity. Once you have settled on a suitable name, conduct thorough research to confirm its uniqueness and avoid potential conflicts with existing businesses. ([Trademark Search](https://www.gov.uk/search-for-trade-mark)) Key Tip: Avoid using misleading names that may confuse customers about the nature of your products or services. Also, refrain from infringing upon another company's registered trademark. Step 2: Notify Relevant Authorities Inform HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) about your intended name change by updating your records online via the Government Gateway account. You must do this within three months of making the change. Additionally, notify Companies House if you are required to file annual accounts or confirmation statements due to being previously registered as a limited liability partnership (LLP), limited company, or LLP designated member. (<https://www.taxation.hmrc.gov.uk/>) Step 3: Update Business Documentation Update all relevant documents reflecting your new trading name, including contracts, invoices, letterheads, websites, social media profiles, email signatures, and promotional materials. Make sure to maintain consistency across all platforms to prevent confusion among clients and suppliers. Step 4: Amend Bank Account Details Contact your bank(s) to update their records with your new trading name. They might require additional information or documentation before processing the request. If necessary, order new checks and debit cards displaying the updated details. Step 5: Register Domains & Social Media Handles Secure matching domain names and social media handles for your new trading name. Consistency across digital channels strengthens brand recognition and makes it easier for customers to find you online. Utilize free tools like Google's [Domain Name Registration Checker](https://domains.google/) and [Knowem](http://knowem.com/) for social media username search. Step 6: Notify Stakeholders Communicate the change effectively to stakeholders, including employees, customers, suppliers, industry associations, and regulatory bodies. Send formal notification emails or letters outlining the reasons behind the rebrand, effective date, and any implications they should be aware of. Encourage questions and provide support during the transition period. Step 7: Monitor Feedback & Address Issues After implementing the changes, monitor feedback from various sources such as customer reviews, social media comments, and internal communications. Be prepared to address concerns promptly and professionally while maintaining transparency throughout the entire process. Conclusion: Changing your trading name requires careful planning, execution, and communication to minimize disruption to your operations and relationships with stakeholders. Following these steps ensures compliance with legal obligations and sets the foundation for a successful rebranding strategy. Remember always to consider professional advice where appropriate and stay consistent in applying the new identity across all aspects of your business. Title: How to Collect Debt From a Friend Introduction: Money can be a touchy subject, especially when it comes to collecting debt from a friend. However, if you find yourself in this situation, it's essential to approach it with sensitivity, respect, and clear communication. This guide will walk you through the process of collecting debt from a friend while maintaining your friendship. Remember that every situation is unique; therefore, adapt these steps as needed based on your circumstances. Step 1: Assess the Situation Before taking any action, evaluate the nature of the debt and your relationship with your friend. Consider the following questions: - Is the debt amount significant or minor? - Have they acknowledged the debt and shown willingness to pay? - What is the current status of your friendship? By answering these questions, you can determine an appropriate course of action and whether it's worth pursuing the debt without causing unnecessary harm to your relationship. Step 2: Communicate Openly Initiate a conversation with your friend about the unpaid debt. Choose a comfortable setting where you both feel at ease discussing financial matters. Be honest but empathetic during the discussion. Avoid accusatory language and focus on finding a solution together. Key Tips: - Clearly state the total owed amount. - Explain why the payment is necessary (e.g., bills, expenses). - Offer flexible repayment options tailored to their financial situation. Step 3: Set Clear Expectations Agree upon a realistic repayment plan that works for both parties. Putting everything in writing can help avoid future misunderstandings. Create a simple agreement outlining the following details: - The total owed amount. - A breakdown of installments, including due dates and amounts. - Consequences for missed payments. - Contact information for any further discussions. Both parties should sign and date the document before finalizing the arrangement. Step 4: Establish a Supportive Tone While collecting debt, maintain a positive attitude towards your friend. Emphasize that you value your relationship above monetary concerns. By creating a supportive environment, you increase the likelihood of successful repayment and preserve your bond. Key Guidelines: - Express gratitude for their efforts to rectify the situation. - Regularly check in on their progress without being overbearing. - Encourage open dialogue regarding challenges or setbacks. Step 5: Monitor Progress Keep track of your friend's repayment schedule to ensure timely payments. If they miss a deadline, remind them gently and reschedule if necessary. Maintaining flexibility demonstrates understanding and fosters trust between you two. Step 6: Seek Professional Help (Optional) If friendly negotiations fail or become too stressful, consider involving a third party such as a mediator or collections agency. Keep in mind that this option may negatively impact your relationship, so exhaust all other avenues first. Before engaging professional services, discuss the potential consequences with your friend and seek legal advice if needed. Conclusion: Collecting debt from a friend requires patience, diplomacy, and effective communication. Approach the issue delicately, focusing on preserving your friendship while recovering the outstanding funds. By following these steps and adapting them to your specific situation, you can navigate the challenging terrain of requesting money from loved ones with grace and integrity. Johnny and his mom were in the park. Johnny skipped around the pathways, scanning for interesting things. He discovered a tiny bug, just a few centimeters long. He was very excited and picked it up carefully. As he watched it, the bug began to shrink! Johnny was amazed and asked his mom to explain. "The bug isn't really shrinking, Johnny," his mom said. "It's playing a trick on you. That's called camouflaging. It's shrinking its body to look like other things around it and protect itself." "It's kind of like when I have to hide behind the couch," Johnny said. His mom laughed. "Exactly," she replied. "Animals are so clever. You have to be careful around them. We have to show them value and respect." Johnny looked at the bug intently. He was full of admiration and respect. "Okay," he said. "I promise to be careful." One of the most mind boggling parts of physics is the very first section - involving projectile motion and the 4 kinematics equations. These equations assume that there is constant acceleration, given by the acceleration of gravity which is the g constant you see in the formulas (9.8 m/s^2). When an object moves through the air (and ignoring the effects of air resistance), it moves in two dimensions - an X and a Y component. The first step is to separate the X and Y, meaning - you can apply those 4 formulas to the X direction (horizontally) and you can also apply them in the Y-direction (vertically). Imagine if you throw a ball sideways off the cliff that is 3m high, and you want to know how long it takes. Since both of the x and y parts are happening at the same time, that means that the Time that you use for both equations is the same. This means conceptually - that if I shot something straight sideways across a field with, let's say, a cannon, it would take the same amount of time to reach the ground as if I had simply dropped the cannonball instead. Weird, isn't it? The reason is that gravity does not affect the horizontal part of the motion, only the vertical part. So the first step is to look at all the equations and pick the one that has most of the elements you have, but is missing the elements you are looking for. X=VoT + 1/2AT^2 is a good choice because you have the height and you want the time. In the vertical Y-direction you can just rewrite this (replacing the X with Y, and replacing the A with g): Y= VoT + 1/2(g)T^2. First thing to note is that in the Y-direction (up and down), there is no Initial Velocity. You did not throw the ball straight UP and you did not push it DOWN when it left your hand, you threw it SIDEWAYS, right? Thus Vo=0 in the vertical direction and the whole VoT becomes equal to 0. You are left with Y=1/2gT^2 and this is where the equation Height(h)= 1/2gt^2 comes from. So here goes: 3 = 1/2gT^2 3 = 0 + (1/2)(9.8)T^2 T^2 = 2*3/9.8 = 0.6122 T = 0.78 sec H=1/2gt^2 can be used in situations where things are flying sideways off cliffs or when simply dropped, like from an airplane..where you did not apply an acceleration to it by lobbing it UP in the air or pushing it DOWN. Remember this equation: (H=1/2gt^2) it will come in handy! And best of luck on your physics exams! Send me an email and let me clarify this and other mind-boggling physics concepts to you or your student! Adjusting colors on an LCD monitor is essential to ensure accurate and vibrant visuals that suit your preferences. This guide will walk you through the process in detail, explaining each step's significance and offering helpful tips along the way. **Part 1: Accessing Color Settings** Step 1: **Turn on your computer.** Make sure your LCD monitor is connected properly and turned on as well. Step 2: **Access Display Settings.** Right-click anywhere on the desktop and select 'Display settings' or 'Personalize.' If using a Mac, go to System Preferences > Displays. Key tip: Some Windows systems may still have the older Control Panel interface (Control Panel > Appearance and Personalization > Display). Both methods lead to similar options but might be organized differently. Step 3: **Select the Correct Display.** If multiple displays are detected, click 'Identify' to label them with numbers; then choose the number corresponding to your LCD monitor. Guideline: Always double-check which display you're adjusting when working with multi-monitor setups. **Part 2: Calibrating Basic Color Settings** Step 4: **Open Color Management.** Click 'Advanced display settings,' scroll down, and hit 'Color calibration.' On macOS, this option can be found under 'Colors' after clicking on your selected display. Step 5: **Begin Calibration Process.** Follow the prompts to start calibrating. You'll typically see a series of screens asking you to match what you see on-screen with preset color patches. Key Tip: Don't rush! Take your time during these steps since accuracy matters more than speed. Also, remember that ambient light conditions can affect perception, so try to maintain consistent lighting throughout the process. Step 6: **Set Gamma Correction.** The gamma setting affects midtones - too high causes washed out images while low values result in darker ones. Match the middle patch closely without worrying about others initially. Step 7: **Adjust Brightness & Contrast.** These controls determine overall image brightness and contrast ratio. Increase brightness if whites appear grayish and decrease it if they glow. For contrast, aim for visible details in both shadows and highlights. Guideline: A balanced contrast level prevents loss of shadow/highlight detail and maintains natural color saturation. **Part 3: Fine-Tuning Advanced Color Settings** Please note that advanced settings vary depending on the graphics card software or operating system. Here we cover general concepts applicable across platforms. Step 8: **Manage Individual Color Channels.** Most interfaces allow separate RGB slider adjustments. Move each channel individually towards neutral grays until all three channels align visually. Key Tip: Small incremental changes often produce better results than drastic shifts. Step 9: **Test Your Changes.** After making adjustments, observe various types of content like photos, videos, websites, etc., to verify whether improvements hold true universally. Guideline: Consistency is crucial; therefore, evaluate different kinds of media rather than focusing solely on one particular type. Remember, everyone's vision varies slightly, so there's no single perfect setup. Instead, strive for subjective satisfaction by creating an environment where visuals look pleasing and accurate according to your unique perspective. Something more than half a century ago, a person, in going along Holborn, might have seen, near the corner of one of the thoroughfares which diverge towards Russell Square, the respectable-looking shop of a glover and haberdasher named James Harvey, a man generally esteemed by his neighbors, and who was usually considered well to do in the world. Like many London tradesmen, Harvey was originally from the country. He had come up to town when a poor lad, to push his fortune, and by dint of steadiness and civility, and a small property left him by a distant relation, he had been able to get into business on his own account, and to attain that most important element of success in London--"a connection." Shortly after setting up in the world, he married a young woman from his native town, to whom he had been engaged ever since his school-days; and at the time our narrative commences he was the father of three children. Title: How to Compile a C/C++ Program on Ubuntu Linux Introduction: In this tutorial, you will learn how to compile a C or C++ program using the GCC (GNU Compiler Collection) on an Ubuntu Linux system. This guide assumes that you have basic familiarity with the terminal and text editing in Linux environment. By following these steps, you'll be able to transform your source code into an executable file. Step 1: Install the Required Tools Firstly, ensure that you have the build-essential package installed, which contains the GCC compiler and other necessary tools. To install this package, open Terminal and type the following command: ```bash sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y build-essential ``` This command updates the local package list and then installs the build-essential package along with its dependencies. Key Tip: Keeping your packages up-to-date is essential for ensuring proper compilation and functionality of your programs. Therefore, running `sudo apt update` before installing any new software is good practice. Step 2: Create Your Source Code File Create a new directory where you want to save your project files. Inside this directory, create a new C/C++ source code file using a text editor like nano, vim, or emacs. For example, let's create a simple 'hello world' C program named hello\_world.c: ```bash nano hello_world.c ``` Add the following content inside hello\_world.c: ```c #include <stdio.h> int main() { printf("Hello World!\n"); return 0; } ``` Save and close the file by pressing `Ctrl+X`, followed by `Y`, and then press Enter. Step 3: Compile Your Source Code Now, you can start compiling your source code file using the gcc compiler. Type the following command in Terminal: I thought I had it all licked. How to Access Windows 8 Games Windows 8 comes with a variety of built-in games that you can access and play right from your desktop or Start screen. This tutorial will guide you through the process of finding and launching these games, as well as provide some helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Open the Start Screen To begin, move your mouse pointer to the lower left corner of the screen, or press the Windows key on your keyboard, to open the Start screen. The Start screen is where all of your apps, including games, are located. Step 2: Search for Games On the Start screen, look for the search charm, which is represented by a magnifying glass icon, usually located at the top right corner of the screen. Click on this icon to open the search bar. Type "games" into the search bar and then click on the "Apps" filter option to narrow down the results to just apps related to gaming. Step 3: View Installed Games From the search results, scroll through the list of installed games on your computer. You may see games that were pre-installed when you purchased your PC, as well as any games you have downloaded from the Microsoft Store. If you don't see a game you were expecting, make sure it is installed on your system and try searching again. Step 4: Launch a Game Once you have found the game you want to play, simply click on its tile to launch it. Some tiles might be live tiles, displaying information such as recent scores or updates; others may be static tiles showing only the game's logo or title. Either way, clicking on the tile will start the game. Key Tips & Guidelines: * To quickly return to the Start screen while playing a game, press the Windows key + D on your keyboard to show the desktop briefly before returning to the Start screen. Alternatively, press the Alt + Tab keys simultaneously to cycle between open applications. * Keep your system up to date with the latest Windows updates to ensure optimal performance of your games and other applications. Updates often include bug fixes, security patches, and improvements to overall stability. * Use the Xbox app (available via the Microsoft Store) to track achievements, connect with friends, and discover new games across multiple devices, including PC and console platforms. * Make sure your graphics card drivers are updated regularly to take advantage of the latest features and optimizations specific to your hardware. Many manufacturers offer automatic driver update tools to simplify this process. * Adjust power settings to prioritize high performance while gaming. Go to Control Panel > Hardware and Sound > Power Options and select the High Performance plan. Note that this setting may increase energy consumption. * Consider using a dedicated gaming controller if one is available for your preferred games. While many titles support keyboard and mouse controls, controllers can provide a more comfortable experience for certain genres like racing or fighting games. * Customize the appearance of the Start screen by resizing, rearranging, or unpinning tiles. Right-click on a tile to reveal context options, such as larger or smaller sizes, or the ability to remove it from the Start screen entirely. The cithara was brought by the Romans to Hispania (Spain) around 40 AD. In the 8th century the Moors brought the four-stringed oud into Spain. The introduction to the oud caused changes to the design of the cithara. In other parts of Europe, the six-string Scandinavian "lut" (lute) became popular wherever the Vikings had been. By 1200 AD, there were two types of the four string "guitar": the "guitarra morisca" (Moorish guitar) from Spain which had a rounded back, wide fingerboard and several soundholes, and the "guitarra latina" (Latin guitar) which was more like the modern guitar with one sound hole and a narrower neck. Title: How to Delete Your CodePen Account Deleting your CodePen account is a straightforward process that can be completed in just a few steps. However, before you proceed with deletion, make sure you understand the implications of this action. Once deleted, all your pens, collections, and profile information will be permanently removed and cannot be recovered. If you still wish to delete your CodePen account, follow these steps: ### Step 1: Log into your CodePen account To begin the deletion process, log into your CodePen account by visiting <https://codepen.io/login>. Enter your email address or username along with your password, then click 'Log in'. If the sum of the proper factors: = the number, then the number is perfect. > the number, then the number is abundant. < the number, then the number is deficient. Today, Bea and Jay learned that if a number is abundant and you can find a set of fewer than all of its proper factors that add up to the number, then the number is called semiperfect. Title: How to Avoid Purchasing Toxic Fruits or Vegetables Introduction: Eating fruits and vegetables is an essential part of maintaining a healthy diet. However, certain practices in farming can lead to the accumulation of toxic substances in these foods. This guide will help you identify and avoid purchasing such produce. Step 1: Understand the Term 'Pesticide' Explanation: A pesticide is any substance used to kill, repel, or control pests (insects, rodents, fungi, etc.) that harm plants or animals. While they protect crops from damage, excessive usage can leave harmful residues on fruits and vegetables. Key Tip: Look out for organic produce which are grown without synthetic pesticides. Step 2: Learn About the 'Dirty Dozen' List Explanation: The Environmental Working Group (EWG) publishes two lists annually - the Dirty Dozen and the Clean Fifteen. The Dirty Dozen includes those fruits and veggies with highest levels of pesticide contamination. Guideline: Regularly check EWG's updated list to know which items should be prioritized when buying organic. Step 3: Prioritize Buying Organic From the Dirty Dozen List Explanation: If possible, always choose organically grown versions of these items to minimize exposure to potentially harmful chemicals. Key Tips: - Shop at local farmers markets where you can directly ask about their farming methods. - Join Community Supported Agriculture (CSA) programs offering organic produce. Step 4: Get Familiar With the 'Clean Fifteen' List Explanation: On the other hand, the Clean Fifteen consists of conventionally grown produce least likely to contain high levels of pesticide residue. Guideline: It's generally safe to buy non-organic varieties of these items if budget constraints prohibit going fully organic. Step 5: Practice Proper Cleaning Techniques Explanation: Even after purchasing organic produce, thorough cleaning is necessary to remove bacteria, dirt, and traces of agricultural chemicals. Instructions: 1. Rinely all fresh produce under running water before eating, peeling, or cutting. 2. Use a vegetable brush for firm produce like melons and cucumbers. 3. For leafy greens, soak them in cold water mixed with white vinegar (1 cup vinegar per gallon of water), then rinse thoroughly. Step 6: Grow Your Own Produce Explanation: One surefire way to ensure your food isn't tainted by pesticides is growing them yourself using natural gardening techniques. Guidelines: - Start small with easy-to-grow herbs and lettuces. - Research Integrated Pest Management strategies that encourage ecological balance rather than chemical intervention. Conclusion: By following this comprehensive guide, you'll significantly reduce the risk of consuming toxic fruits and vegetables, thereby promoting healthier living through informed choices. The trouble is well it's like this barrel of apples... Delete Your MySpace Profile: A Comprehensive Guide =============================================== MySpace was once a popular social networking platform; however, many users have since moved on to other platforms like Facebook, Instagram, or Twitter. If you're one of those who no longer uses MySpace and want to delete your profile permanently, this guide will walk you through the process step-by-step. Before we begin, here are some important points to consider: * Deleting your MySpace profile is permanent and cannot be undone. Make sure you really want to delete your account before proceeding. * You must be logged into your MySPACE account to delete your profile. * It may take up to 30 days for your data to be completely removed from their servers after deletion. Step 1: Log in to Your MySpace Account ------------------------------------- Visit <https://www.myspace.com/> and click on 'Log in' at the top right corner of the page. Enter your email address and password associated with your MySpace account. Once logged in, hover over your profile picture located at the upper right-hand side of the screen, then select "Settings" from the dropdown menu. Key Tip: Save any valuable information (photos, messages) that you wish to keep before proceeding as they will be deleted along with your profile. Why This Step Matters: To successfully delete your MySpace profile, you need to access your account settings which can only be done when you log in. Step 2: Access Account Settings ------------------------------ Under the 'Account' tab within settings, scroll down until you see the section labeled 'Deactivate'. Click on the link saying 'If you still want to deactivate your account, click here.' Why This Step Matters: The 'Deactivate' option allows you to temporarily disable your account but keeps all your content intact if you ever decide to return. However, choosing to delete removes everything permanently. Step 3: Initiate Permanent Deletion ---------------------------------- You'll now see an alert box asking if you're sure about deleting your account. Check the boxes next to both statements confirming that you understand the implications of this action. Then, click on the red button marked 'delete myspace account'. Key Guideline: Read carefully what you agree to by checking these two boxes. They state that you acknowledge that this action is irreversible and that you allow MySpace to collect anonymous data even after deletion. Why This Step Matters: By clicking this button, you initiate the actual deletion process of your MySpace profile. Step 4: Confirm Email Address ----------------------------- Check your registered email address for a message from MySpace titled 'Confirm Account Deactivation Request'. Open it and click on the confirmation link provided inside. Why This Step Matters: This verification step ensures that it's actually you requesting the deletion of your account, preventing accidental or unauthorized removal. Step 5: Wait for Confirmation Email ----------------------------------- After clicking the confirmation link, wait for another email from MySpace stating that your account has been scheduled for deletion. Please note that it might take several weeks for them to fully remove your data due to technical reasons. And there you have it! You've successfully initiated the deletion of your MySpace profile. Remember, once completed, this process cannot be reversed so ensure you truly want to part ways with MySpace before taking these steps. Look at the picture you can make. SHOPPING AND MONEY VALUES LESSON IDEA Here is a lesson idea from one of our teaching members: - For students to learn the value of money. - ESL students to learn the value of the different coins - The value of food - The concepts of 2 for $2.00 etc. - The use of decimals. I used the following lesson with my beginner 3rd Grade ESL students. It may be modified for other grade levels. - Each Student gets a store Supermarket Flyer. sheet of paper Marker Classroom play Register Classroom Money or real money. Students must choose at least two items from each food group. Students prepare a shopping list. They must add the total of their items. They are each given a certain amount of money to spend. This depends on the math concept being taught. For example: They may not have enough so: "How much more do you need?" or "What is the change?" etc. Students must calculate their change so they can Each student can take a turn at the register. The shoppers come to the register with their list and their money. The "cashier" must give the correct change. *With beginner ESL students, this lesson may take a week. Title: Downloading YouTube Videos Using MediaConverter Introduction: Downloading videos from YouTube can be quite useful for various reasons such as offline viewing, creating mashups or backups. This tutorial will guide you through downloading YouTube videos using MediaConverter, an online media conversion tool that supports multiple formats including MP4, MP3, AVI, FLV, and more. It's free, easy-to-use, and doesn't require any software installation. Step 1: Open Your Web Browser Open your preferred web browser (Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge, etc.) on your computer or laptop. Key Tip: Make sure your internet connection is stable throughout this process. A poor connection might interrupt the conversion and downloading process. Step 2: Navigate to MediaConverter Website In the address bar at the top of your browser window, type mediaconverter.org and press Enter. You'll land on their homepage where all the magic happens! Guideline: Always ensure you are accessing the correct URL to avoid potential security risks associated with malicious websites disguised as legitimate ones. Step 3: Locate & Copy Video URL Go to YouTube and open the video you wish to download. Highlight the entire URL visible in the address bar, right click and select 'Copy'. Key Tips: Only download content if it complies with copyright laws and terms of service of both YouTube and MediaConverter. Avoid infringing upon creator rights. Step 4: Paste URL into MediaConverter Return to Mediaconverter.org tab still opened in your browser. Directly below the text reading "Paste Link Here," left click and choose 'Paste.' The URL should appear automatically after clicking. Step 5: Choose Desired Output Format Just beneath the input box, there's a dropdown menu labeled 'Convert To.' Click on it and select your preferred format - most commonly used are MP4 for video and MP3 for audio. Step 6: Adjust Quality Settings Beside the 'Convert To' option, there's another dropdown titled 'Quality.' Higher quality usually means larger file size but better resolution. Select according to your preference and device compatibility. Step 7: Initiate Conversion Process Click the blue button marked 'Start,' located towards the bottom right corner of the page. Wait while MediaConverter processes your request which includes extracting the video from YouTube server then converting it into your chosen format. Step 8: Download Converted File Once completed, you'll see a green 'Download' button next to your converted file details. Left click it and then hit 'Save As' when prompted by your browser. Name the file appropriately and save it in your desired location. Key Guideline: Be cautious about saving files from unknown sources directly onto your desktop or primary folders without renaming them, as they could overwrite existing important documents unintentionally. Conclusion: Congratulations! By following these steps meticulously, you have successfully downloaded a YouTube video via MediaConverter. Remember always respect intellectual property rights and only download content legally available for public usage. Enjoy watching or listening to your favorite clips offline! Um once there's a little boy waking up. 2003 Virginia SOLs Motivation for Learning Questions to ask students: An air column will vibrate with a wavelength dependent on the length of the tube, and, therefore, a certain pitch. The wavelength cannot be seen of course, but it can be seen that the longer the air column, the lower the pitch. When you blow across a bottle with water in it, the vibrating medium is the column of air whose length ends at the top of the water. Therefore, the more water that is in the bottle, the shorter the column of air, and the higher the pitch. When the same bottle is struck with a mallet, the vibrating medium is the glass and the water. The more water that is in the bottle, the more vibrating material there is (longer column), and the lower the pitch when struck with a mallet. To print out the Student Copy only, click here. When you blow on the end of the straw, the two pieces of the tip vibrate together. This makes a vibration, which is necessary to make sound. The tips vibrate at a specific frequency and wavelength that is determined by the length of the straw. The vibration travels down the straw and reflects from the end. This sets up a wave in the air in the straw; the vibration will bounce back and forth between the two ends. It is this vibration that you are hearing. Changing the length of the straw (by clipping it off, or by making a straw trombone) changes the wavelength and frequency, and so changes the pitch. Making a hole in the straw so it is like a real instrument lets the vibration bounce off from where the hole is, which will also change the pitch. Similarly, hitting the pipe causes it to vibrate, and the frequency and wavelength depend on the length. Have the students compare straws and pipes of different widths. Have them try different designs for the straw's tip. Which one works best? Does it make a difference how hard you blow into the straw? Tune the instruments to simple notes, and have the students perform an easy song, such as "Mary Had a Little Lamb." You could also explain how vocal chords use the same principle to make different pitches. Students with Special Needs Many students may not be able to get a noise out of the straw oboes. These students can cut the straws while the other students are blowing through them. They can also adjust the "trombone slide" as another student is playing the oboe. Click here for further information on laboratories with students with special needs. The magma is forced between other layers of rock by the pressure under the Earth's surface. The magma cools and turns slowly into solid stone. Granite has many different types of minerals in it. Copper is one of the few coloured metals. Most metals are gray or silver. Gold, copper, caesium and osmium are the only four coloured metals. Copper is green as copper(II) carbonate and copper(II) hydroxide. It turns green because it oxidises. After a time in the air, copper forms green copper carbonate at the surface, called verdigris. That is why the copper roof of a building looks green. Let's see what else you can find. Scurvy is a disease (sickness). It is caused by not eating enough vitamin C. But scurvy can be prevented. It has many symptoms. People who have scurvy get spots on their skin, especially on their legs. Their teeth may loosen and/or fall out. They may bleed from the mouth, nose, and gums (mucus membranes). A person with scurvy will look pale and feel sad (see depression). They will not be able to move easily, because their joints hurt. I guess it's kind of a donkey? [moves silver man toward construction; touches it to a silver man on the construction; that silver man moves slightly] Understanding how we hear starts with understanding how the ear works. There are three parts to the ear, outer ear, middle ear and inner ear. The outer ear consists of the pinna (the part of the ear we can see on the side of the head) and the ear canal. The eardrum is located at the end of the ear canal. The main job of the outer ear is to collect sound waves and send them down the ear canal to the eardrum. The middle ear includes the eardrum and three small bones. They are located in an air filled space surrounded by the bones of the skull. Their job is to amplify the sound waves and send them to the inner ear. The inner ear is located in a snail shaped bone deep inside the head. This boney section is filled with a fluid. The hearing nerves called haircells are in this part of the ear. Sound travels through the inner ear by setting up waves in the fluid. These waves "wash" over the haircells, stimulating them. The stimulated nerve fibers change sound into electrical impulses that travel to the brain. It is the brain that assigns meaning to the sounds and words. We hear in the brain. It recognizes familiar voices and birds, understands words and enjoys music. Let's see if we can have both. They do kind of look like bear passengers they all have ears. The color violet (the bottom or innermost color of the rainbow) has a wavelength of about 400 nm ("nanometers") which is 0.00004 centimeters or 0.000016 inches. Photons with wavelengths of 10-400 nm are called ultraviolet (or UV) light. Such light cannot be seen by the human eye. On the other end of the spectrum, red light is about 700 nm. Infrared light is about 700 nm to 300,000 nm. Human eyes are not sensitive to infrared light either. It is similar to the original model, but it has a "clamshell" design, meaning that it can be folded to open and close which was later used in all the DS and 3DS models except the original 2DS. The Game Boy Advance SP has a backlit screen, meaning it has a bright screen (older versions of the GBA SP have a frontlit screen). Fluency-Stride through Reading Rational: In helping children read with fluency, it is harder than one thinks. For children, it was once a challenge and still is for some to just pronounce the words correctly, now we are asking them to read with fluency. Fluency is developed after children become automatic readers with the words they see. Reading fluently is not about the faster reader, it is more about reading with automatic word recognition This is what helps get the message across. In this exercise, the children will be working with a partner to record each other's reading by marking words that get stumbled or mispronounced. This will allow the students to hear what it sounds like when they stumble over a word, therefore making them more cautious when reading. We will discuss as that it is not about the faster reader, it is about reading at a pace that keeps the interest in the story. -Book-Up the Hill by Matt Sims -There should be enough copies of chapter 1 and 2 for all the pairs of students -Stopwatch (enough for all pairs) -Fluency bar graph chart (Chart that monitors the growth of the readings; include space for name, 5 reading times, and 5 reading errors) 1."Today students we are going to work on reading smoothly with fluency." "Fluency is not about the fastest reader, because trying to speed through the reading causes you to cut or mispronounce words. When reading, you must read smoothly and allow the words that are being read to sound as if they are just being said, like a regular conversation. I proceed to read a page from Up the Hill. "First I am going to read to you stumbling over my words." "J J Jan r ran up da hil" Then I will read the same passage with fluency to show you all the difference. "Jan ran up the hill" "How did I improve?" Did you understand me better" "Well I want us to practice your reading so that each and every one of you will read fluently and with expression." 2. I will provide another oral example on the board by writing a sentence for us to practice reading fluently. Billy and Jack are best friends. "Billy and Jack are best friends. "Now I would like for you to practice reading while sounding out each word and smoothing the sentence. "Did this sentence some together and make sense to you?" "Ok, I am going to read it to you super fast." BillynJac r besfriens" "Could you understand what I was saying?" "It was not clear" "Now I want us to read it one more time with fluency!" 3. I am going to explain to the students that they will be paired up with a partner, and they are going to read a chapter from Up the Hill, until they are reading it fluently. "You and your partner will both read the chapter. When one is reading, the other will start the clock and begin marking words that the partner stumbles or mispronounces. You both will do this for as many times possible in 20 minutes. Each reading will last for 1 minute, so at 1 minute, stop the stopwatch and tell your partner where they made their mistakes so they will know the next time they read." "I will be walking around helping you all if words are too too hard" I will show you how to do this. "Jack and I will show you an example. Jack began reading as I start the stopwatch" Ok, Now Jack you are done, you mispronounced and stumbled over these words here so next time try to correct them. Now I am going to write down the time in which he got finished. Now I will read and Jack will do the same for me. 4. The students are going to break into their pairs. Each student will be given a copy of Up the Hill (chapters 1&2). They will now monitor each other and began recording mistakes and times. By the end the times should have improved as well as the fluency of the students. Assessment: I will assess the students by walking around in the end to hear each of them read for one minute individually to test their performance and improvement. While listening, I will also be looking at their record sheet to see if they have improved throughout the lesson. [aft returns with an Uncle Remus book] Why the moon is red-orange sometimes? It is the same reason that the sun is red at sunset and sunrise. Blue light is scattered more strongly by haze, soot, and other aerosols than red light is, so if there is haze, etc. in the air the red light will come through more strongly. Note that this effect is strongest when the moon is nearest the horizon, when the moonlight has to pass through a greater thickness of air. The moon is also red during lunar eclipses. This is because the only light that reaches it has passed through the edges of the earth's atmosphere. Title: How to Host a Memorable and Enjoyable Party =============================================== Introduction: A well-hosted party can create lasting memories and strengthen relationships with friends, family, or colleagues. This guide will provide you with comprehensive steps and helpful tips to ensure your event runs smoothly, leaving guests feeling satisfied and entertained. Step 1: Define Your Vision and Objectives ---------------------------------------- ### Explanation Begin by identifying the purpose of your gathering. Is it a casual get-together, birthday celebration, holiday party, or formal dinner? Understanding your objectives enables appropriate planning, resource allocation, and mood setting. ### Key Tips * Determine the number of attendees based on available space and budget. * Choose a theme that aligns with the occasion and appeals to your target audience. ## Step 2: Plan the Logistics ### Explanation Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved music and always wanted to play the flute. One day, her mommy bought her a flute and she was so happy. Lily tried to play it, but it didn't sound good. She asked her mommy why it didn't sound like the music she heard. Her mommy said, "You have to examine the flute and see if everything is okay." Lily looked at the flute and found out that one of the holes was covered. She fixed it and tried to play again. This time, it sounded beautiful! She was so successful in playing the flute. The moral of the story is that if you want to do something, you have to examine everything to make sure it's right. If you do that, you can be successful in anything you want to do. [father repeating conversations between Mark and Ross.] [8 sec, noise of moving highchair, traffic, etc.] Owls have special adaptations which help them swivel their heads 270 degrees. They have 14 neck vertebrae instead of our seven. Also, the big carotid arteries, instead of being on the side of the neck as in humans, are carried close to the centre of rotation just in front of the spine. So these arteries get much less twisting and stretching, and the potential for damage is greatly reduced. This arrangement is seen in other birds, but in owls the vertebral arteries - the vessels that travel through channels in the neck bones - are given extra space. [this is referring to the trunk lid of the Toyota station wagon] Title: How to Explain a Termination in a Job Interview Introduction: Job interviews can be stressful, especially when you have to explain why you were terminated from a previous job. However, with proper preparation and understanding of what to say and how to say it, you can turn this potential negative into a positive opportunity to showcase your growth and professionalism. This tutorial will provide an in-depth guide on how to effectively explain a termination during a job interview. Step 1: Stay Calm and Honest It's essential to approach the topic calmly and honestly. Employers appreciate honesty and transparency, so avoid being defensive or making excuses. Instead, acknowledge the situation and express regret if necessary. By staying composed, you demonstrate maturity and self-awareness, which are valuable qualities for any potential employer. Key tip: Practice deep breathing exercises beforehand to help maintain composure during the conversation. Step 2: Prepare Your Response Beforehand Anticipate the question about your termination and prepare a thoughtful response ahead of time. Reflecting on the reasons behind your departure and crafting a clear, concise explanation allows you to present yourself confidently and articulately. Guideline: Start by briefly outlining the circumstances surrounding your termination, taking responsibility where appropriate, and then highlighting what you learned from the experience and how it has helped you grow professionally. Step 3: Focus on Lessons Learned Emphasize the lessons you gained from the termination rather than dwelling on the negative aspects. Highlight specific skills or knowledge that you acquired as a result of the experience, such as improved communication, conflict resolution, or adapting to change. Key tip: Use language like "I realized" or "I discovered," emphasizing personal growth and reflection. Step 4: Connect Your Learnings to Future Opportunities Link the lessons you've learned to future opportunities within the role you're applying for. Showcasing how these experiences make you a better candidate demonstrates resilience and adaptability, traits most employers value highly. Example: "As a result of my past employment, I am now more adept at managing competing priorities under tight deadlines while maintaining high-quality work." Step 5: Keep It Brief Limit your explanation to a few sentences; there is no need to go into great detail. Overexplaining may come across as unprofessional or evasive. Stick to the facts, take responsibility where needed, and focus on moving forward positively. Key guideline: Avoid blaming others or casting doubt on your former employer's decisions. Maintaining respect for all parties involved shows emotional intelligence and professionalism. Step 6: Redirect the Conversation Toward Positive Topics Once you've addressed the termination, pivot the discussion toward other topics, focusing on your strengths, achievements, and goals. Demonstrating enthusiasm for new challenges highlights your motivation and eagerness to contribute positively to a team. Key tip: Have examples ready of times when you successfully navigated difficult situations or overcame obstacles in previous roles. These stories illustrate your problem-solving abilities and capacity for learning from setbacks. Conclusion: Being terminated from a job doesn't define your career trajectory. With careful planning, self-reflection, and strategic messaging, you can transform a potentially awkward moment into an opportunity to display your professional development and commitment to improvement. Remember to stay calm, truthful, and focused on the positive outcomes of your experiences. oh, this is a cute toy Caroline look. Uh no you're going to go after more pizza okay. Philosophy can be divided into different groups, based on the types of questions that it asks. Below is a list of questions split into groups. One possible list of answers to these questions can be called a 'philosophy'. There are many different 'philosophies', because all of these questions have many different answers according to different people. Not all philosophies ask the same questions. I'd say that he has a runny nose. Ever heard someone say something like, "I feel like I've been waiting light years for this line to move?" It's a common mistake; one that is made daily by multitudes of people. A light year is not a measure of time but a measure of distance. That doesn't make much sense because "light year" contains the word "year", which is normally a unit of time. Even so, light years measure distance. It is the distance light travels unobstructed in a year, to be precise. You know how long a foot or a meter is - you are comfortable with these units because you use them every day. Same thing with miles and kilometers - these are nice, human increments of distance. When astronomers use their telescopes to look at stars, things are different. The distances are gigantic. For example, the closest star to Earth (besides our sun) is something like 24,000,000,000,000 miles (38,000,000,000,000 kilometers) away. That's the closest star. There are stars that are billions of times farther away than that. When you start talking about those kinds of distances, a mile or kilometer just isn't a practical unit to use because the numbers get too big. No one wants to write or talk about numbers that have 20 digits in them! So to measure really long distances, people use a unit called a light year. The speed of light is equal to 186,000 miles (300,000 kilometers) per second (also known as a light-second), and therefore a light year is 5,865,696,000,000 miles, or 9,460,800,000,000 kilometers (186,000 miles/second x 60 seconds/minute x 60 minutes/hour x 24 hours/day x 365 days/year = 5,865,696,000,000 miles/year). The average distance from the sun to the Earth is referred to as an astronomical unit (AU), which is 92,955,807 miles (149,597,870,700 meters). However, to measure longer distances, astronomers use light years. Using a light year as a distance measurement has another advantage; it helps you determine age. Let's say that a star is 1 million light years away. The light from that star has traveled at the speed of light to reach us. Therefore, it has taken the star's light 1 million years to get here, and the light we are seeing was created 1 million years ago. So the star we are seeing is really how the star looked a million years ago, not how it looks today. In the same way, we are less than 8.5 light minutes from the sun. If the sun were to suddenly explode right now, we wouldn't know about it for eight minutes because that is how long it would take for the light of the explosion to get here. Neptune, our solar system's current farthest planet, is 30.06 AU (or 2,794,251,558 est. avg. miles/ 4,504,000,000 km) from the sun. It only takes the sun's light a little over 4 hours to reach Neptune. And on the outer edges of the solar system, the Oort Cloud, where comets are thought to originate, is 100,000 AU from the sun. I should probably not start her out that way. [car or truck going by drowns out Chi's voice] mm, must be shinin' out the window, huh? It was 1792 when Theodore Pearson of Newburyport, Massachusetts, made a cracker-like bread that was made from only water and flour which he called "Pearson's Pilot Bread." This was the first cracker bakery in the United States, and made crackers for more than a century. And Winnie-the-Pooh went to a very muddy place that he knew of. Let's see some of these other animals. Are we all set up to go swimming? Chemical elements are given a unique "chemical symbol". Chemical symbols are used all over the world. This means that, no matter which language is spoken, there is no confusion about what the symbol means. Chemical symbols of elements almost always come from their English or Latin names. For example, carbon has the chemical symbol 'C', and sodium has chemical symbol 'Na', after the Latin "natrium". Tungsten is called 'W' after its German name, "wolfram". 'Au' is the symbol for gold and it comes from the Latin word for gold, "aurum". Another symbol which comes from Latin is 'Ag'. This is the element silver and it comes from the Latin "argentum". Lead's symbol, 'Pb', comes from the Latin "plumbum" and the English word plumber derives from this as pipes used to be made out of lead. Some more recently discovered elements were named after famous people, like einsteinium, which was named after Albert Einstein. Lily and Tom were playing in the park with their toys. Daylight Savings is upon us already! What will you do in your extra hour? The concept of "telling time" can be tricky for kids to comprehend-but that doesn't mean they can't have fun while learning! And what better way to explore these concepts than during Daylight Savings? If you're looking for some easy, fun ways to help your child explore time, grab a clock (real or pretend) and choose from the six activities listed below. That extra hour will fly by before you know it! 1) The Fundamentals: What IS a Clock, Anyway? Before your child begins to understand the concepts of "telling time," talk about what the different parts of a clock are, and how they all come together. For example: count the number of hands and compare their length; talk about how many hours are represented on the clock, and how many minutes; or show the child how the hands move in a clockwise direction. 2) Matching Numbers with Hours: One Step at a Time Point both hands toward the 12. Using your pretend clock (or a real one that is flexible enough to be manually changed), ask the child to move the minute hand around the clock one time. Explain that when the minute hand goes all the way around on a real clock, it means an hour has passed. Ask the child to move the hour hand to the 1 to show that one hour has passed. Repeat the activity, letting the child move the hour hand to the next number with each minute-hand rotation. 3) Making Sense of "Minutes" Has your child ever said, "It feels like I've been waiting for 1,000 minutes!" Help your child understand what a "minute" or "hour" really is by discussing the concepts of duration and lengths of time. For instance, you might tell the child how many minutes he or she brushes teeth, or how long it takes to drive to a grandparent's house. 4) Tell a "Time Story" To help your child understand the concept of time passage, you might want to discuss a trip to the playground this way: "We go to the playground in the morning. It's 10:00 when we get to the playground. We play for one hour. It's 11:00 when we leave. It takes us five minutes to walk back to the car. It takes us ten minutes to drive home. When we get home, it's lunchtime." Discuss various different story scenarios with your child that relate to daily activities. 5) Draw Your Day Color in this clock-face printable with the things your family does at different times of day, placing the different activities next to their correct times. Then complete the clock: Cut a plastic straw into two unequal pieces (the hour hand and minute hand). Have an adult press a straight pin through the ends of both straw pieces, and then through the center of the paper clock. Secure with a pencil eraser. Spin the hands to talk about your day! (Download the full-size printable here.) 6) What Time Is It? (Who Cares?) With all the structure kids have in their lives (playdates, school, classes, mealtimes, etc.) why not use today as an opportunity to "throw time away" and just play? Ask your child what they would do if time wasn't an option, or hide all the clocks in your house for an afternoon. (You might find this to be as much fun as your child does!) (Thank you to Instagram fan @Sofiasideas for the inspiration behind Activity #6 in the photo below, which features our Wooden Shape Sorting Clock!) How do you explore the concept of time with your child? Share your ideas and activities in the comments below, or on Facebook! Okay you want to put a comma after their names? Shortwave radio frequencies are also reflected from an electrically charged layer of the upper atmosphere, called the Ionosphere. The waves can bounce between the ionosphere and the earth to reach receivers that are not in the line of sight because of the curvature of the Earth's surface. They can reach very far, sometimes around the world. that even when praetor or consul he drank the same wine as his slaves; that a dinner never cost him from the market above thirty pence; and that he was thus frugal for the sake of his country, that he might be able to endure the harder service in war. He adds that having got, among some goods he was heir to, a  piece of Babylonian tapestry, he sold it immediately; that the walls of his country houses were neither plastered nor white-washed; that he never gave more for a slave than fifteen hundred drachmae, as not requiring in his servants delicate shapes and fine faces but strength and ability to labour, that they might be fit to be employed in his stables, about his cattle or on such-like business; and that he thought proper to sell them again when they grew old, that he might have no useless persons to maintain. In a word he thought nothing cheap that was superfluous, that what a man has no need of is dear even at a penny; and that it is much better to have fields where the plough goes and cattle feed, than gardens and walks that require much watering and sweeping. This stern simplicity he carried throughout his life and in words of eloquence (he  was one of the most powerful speakers in Rome) he tried to get others to imitate him. Longitude & SCO X-1 Lets compare the location of Sco X-1 on the black and white map to the location of Sco X-1 on this diagram. Where is it on the black and white map? It is in the center, above the galactic plane. Does this mean that it is not in the Milky Way? Not necessarily. Look at the location of Sco X-1 in the Degrees Longitude diagram. It is nearly at 0o. It is in the direction of the galactic center; similarly, it is near the center of the black and white map. Do you see now why it seems to be so far above the galactic plane? It is very close to the Earth. The latitude of Sco X-1 is about 24o. Where would you draw it on the Degrees Latitude diagram? Longitude & the Crab Nebula Now lets compare the locations of the Crab Nebula. On the black and white map, it is the bright orange source all the way to the right. On the Degrees Longitude diagram, it is near 180o. Now imagine yourself standing on Earth and looking at the galactic center. Suppose you want to then look at the Crab Nebula. You would have to either go to the other side of the Earth to see it (because you can't see through the Earth!), or you would have to wait for the Earth to spin around so that you are facing the opposite direction. Why do you think you won't be able to see the Crab Nebula if you are looking toward the galactic center? (hint: compare the location of the Crab on each diagram: Degrees Longitude, Degrees Latitude diagram and Black and White map) Wrapping It Up Imagine the night sky as a piece of black paper that is wrapped around the Earth. The seam of the paper (where the edges are taped together) is at 180o. The Crab Nebula would be close to the seam since it is close to 180o. Imagine someone pulling the tape off and laying the paper flat on a table. Now where would the Crab be? It would be near the right side of the paper, just as it is on the black and white map! Title: How to Beat Two Garradors in Resident Evil 4 Resident Evil 4 is a popular survival horror game that requires strategy, skill, and quick reflexes to overcome its challenging enemies. One such enemy is the Garrador, a hulking, blind creature with extendable claws. This tutorial will focus on beating two Garradors simultaneously, which can be an intimidating task even for experienced players. By following these steps meticulously, you'll increase your chances of success significantly. **Step 1: Prepare Yourself** Before engaging the Garradors, ensure you are well-equipped. We recommend having the following items: - **Stun Gun:** The Stun Gun temporarily stuns enemies, giving you precious seconds to attack or escape. It's incredibly effective against Garradors. - **Shotgun or Magnum:** These powerful firearms can deal significant damage to the Garradors. The Shotgun has a wider spread, useful at close range, while the Magnum offers more precision and longer reach. - **First Aid Sprays:** Healing is crucial during this battle. Make sure you have enough First Aid Sprays to last throughout the fight. Key Tip: Save often! You don't want to lose progress if things go south. **Step 2: Lure Them Apart** Garradors usually appear in pairs. To avoid being overwhelmed, separate them. Use objects like explosive barrels or thrown knives to distract one as you engage the other. Remember, they're blind but can still hear and sense vibrations. Guideline: Always keep moving when dealing with multiple Garradors. Standing still could prove fatal. **Step 3: Stun One Garrador** Once separated, target one Garrador and aim for their head using your most potent weapon (shotgun or magnum). Ideally, you should try to stun them with the Stun Gun instead. A successful hit will cause the Garrador to fall momentarily, providing a brief window of opportunity. Key Tip: Timing is everything. Wait until the last possible moment before dodging to maximize damage dealt. **Step 4: Attack While They're Down** While the stunned Garrador is recovering, quickly move towards it and unleash several shots into its body. If you timed your shot correctly, you might be able to decapitate it, ending the fight instantly. However, if it gets back up, swiftly dodge its attacks and repeat Step 3. Guideline: Focus on disabling rather than killing immediately. Disabled Garradors won't attack you, buying you time. **Step 5: Repeat Steps 3 & 4 With Second Garrador** After incapacitating the first Garrador, turn your attention to the second one. Follow the same process - stun, attack, dodge. Keep repeating this cycle until both Garradors are defeated. Key Tip: Prioritize safety over speed. Taking unnecessary risks may lead to taking unnecessary damage. Remember, practice makes perfect. Don't be discouraged if you fail initially; learn from your mistakes and improve upon them. Good luck, and happy gaming! And, and, and, and Sabina got the big boat.
